3A_Bibliographic.citation: Karcher, N. R., Klaunig, M. J., Elsayed, N. M., Taylor, R. L., Jay, S. Y., & Schiffman, J. (2022). Understanding associations between race/ethnicity, experiences of discrimination, and psychotic-like experiences in middle childhood. Journal of the American Academy of Child and Adolescent Psychiatry, 61(10), 1262-1272.
3A_Bibliographic.journal: Journal of the American Academy of Child and Adolescent Psychiatry

3B_Sample.timepoints_used: Baseline (psychotic-like experiences) and 1-year follow-up (experiences of discrimination)

3C_Exposure.exposure_measure: N/A - this paper does not examine screen time
3C_Exposure.how_operationalized: N/A - exposure is race/ethnicity and experiences of discrimination (Perceived Discrimination Scale); outcome is psychotic-like experiences (Prodromal Questionnaire-Brief Child Version)

3D_Outcome.measure_used: Prodromal Questionnaire-Brief Child Version (PQ-BC), 21-item youth self-report

3E_Design_Analysis.covariates_listed: Age, sex, English language proficiency, born in US, financial adversity, ACEs, social support

3I_Flags.exclusion_flag: EXCLUDE - This paper does not examine screen time, social media, or smartphone use in any capacity. It examines race/ethnicity and experiences of discrimination as predictors of psychotic-like experiences.
3I_Flags.ambiguity_notes: This paper has no relevance to screen time or social media. It should be excluded from the systematic review.
